behavior_summary: This script implements Salkind, an NPC with: a 'U8P_SALKIND::look' short-description, area-triggered idle behavior/schedules, reactions to theft and being targeted, and an interactive conversation menu.

U8P_SALKIND::look supplies the short description shown when the player examines Salkind, choosing among name/title variants and dead/alive phrasing depending on salkindName, salkindJob, and Npc::isDead(this).

U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea and U8P_SALKIND::leaveFastArea clear internal refs on area entry/exit; U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea additionally dispatches U8P_SALKIND::schedule-specific utilities (U8P_SALKIND::func04B6 or U8P_SALKIND::func05F7) based on the SalkindSchedule global flag unless gated by toranDead, secondexecution, or NPC death.

U8P_SALKIND::func04B6 and U8P_SALKIND::func05F7 are U8P_SALKIND::schedule utilities that perform randomized idle behavior: U8P_SALKIND::func04B6 searches/attempts pathfinds to targets and triggers short monologues or delayed retries; U8P_SALKIND::func05F7 runs a smaller randomized sequence of calls and timers for an alternate U8P_SALKIND::schedule.

U8P_SALKIND::func072E is a larger idle/monologue routine that picks from many barks based on distance, item state and random buckets; U8P_SALKIND::func0DA5 is a small targeted bark ('Who keeps opening these?').
U8P_SALKIND::AvatarStoleSomething is the theft reaction: when active it sets salkindPissed, prepares the NPC (METHOD::func091C), barks an accusation, and triggers follow-on behavior.
U8P_SALKIND::cast handles being targeted/attacked: waits briefly for a target, possibly calls METHOD::func15E7 and CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime, and issues panic barks chosen randomly.

U8P_SALKIND::use builds a dynamic conversation menu (local3), presents choices to the player, captures the selection, updates flags (e.g., salkindMet, salkindName), and modifies the menu according to state (adds/removes topics such as Devon/Bentic lines).
notable_control_flow: U8P_SALKIND::look: selects among textual variants by testing salkindName and salkindJob flags, then Npc::isDead(this) to choose 'dead' vs 'alive' wording.

U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea: early-exits if toranDead set, NPC dead, or secondexecution set; otherwise resetRef() is called to clear internal refs and then U8P_SALKIND::schedule-specific helper is invoked based on SalkindSchedule (0x01 -> U8P_SALKIND::func04B6, 0x02 -> U8P_SALKIND::func05F7).

U8P_SALKIND::func04B6: loop-like iteration over candidate targets, Item::getFrame then Npc::pathfind -> suspend -> branch on process_result(); on success either call U8P_SALKIND::func0DA5 for a specific index or U8P_SALKIND::func072E for idle talk (only if somebodyTalking is false); spawn timers with FREE::func1C19 for retries/delays on different branches.

U8P_SALKIND::cast: retry/wait loop that spawns FREE::func1C19 while no target and a counter > 0, then checks Npc::getTarget result to decide whether to call METHOD::func15E7 and alert guards, then choose a panic bark via urandom.

U8P_SALKIND::use: constructs menu array local3 based on flags (salkindMet, salkindPissed, devonJailed, metDevonInJail), presents it (Ask + SuspendAssign into local2), strcmp on the choice to set flags and add/remove menu entries; loop continues until player picks 'bye'.
open_questions: Exact semantic contents and types of globals like salkindName and salkindJob (appear to be boolean/string presence flags) — their storage format is not explicit in the snippet.
The full semantics of METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7: they appear to prepare speaking state and handle reaction bookkeeping respectively, but exact side-effects are inferred not proven.

Whether SALKIND::func021B referenced by U8P_SALKIND::AvatarStoleSomething is exactly U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea (process type 0x021B) or another internal continuation — mapping inferred from process type IDs but not proven.
Precise timing values and how FREE::func1C19 delays are chosen across branches (only numeric expressions visible; meaning of those timers in gameplay pacing is inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_SALKIND::look
main_function.purpose: Return the NPC short description when the player examines Salkind, selecting among name/title variants and using dead/alive phrasing.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or inspects the NPC Salkind.
Branches within depend on runtime flags salkindName and salkindJob and on Npc::isDead(this).

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::look
function_map.role: Provide 'U8P_SALKIND::look' short description for Salkind; display different text depending on name/job presence and death state.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::schedule
function_map.role: NPC scheduling entry point (placeholder for routine movement/timing).

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry handler: reset internal refs and dispatch U8P_SALKIND::schedule utilities based on SalkindSchedule unless gated.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit handler: clear refs / cleanup.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Theft reaction: set salkindPissed, call preparation method, bark at the player, and trigger follow-up behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::cast
function_map.role: Handle being targeted/attacked: wait/retry for target, call reaction bookkeeping, notify guards, and issue panic barks.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::func04B6
function_map.role: Schedule utility: iterate candidate targets, attempt pathfinds, and trigger short monologues or retries based on results.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::func05F7
function_map.role: Alternative U8P_SALKIND::schedule utility: randomized short sequences and timers for a different U8P_SALKIND::schedule state.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::func072E
function_map.role: Idle/monologue routine with many barks chosen by distance, item state, and randomness.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::func0DA5
function_map.role: Short targeted monologue and possible door-U8P_SALKIND::use spawn: 'Who keeps opening these?'.

function_map.name: U8P_SALKIND::use
function_map.role: Conversation handler: build menu, present options, capture choice, and update flags and menu entries accordingly.

internal_calls.purpose: Run alternate U8P_SALKIND::schedule behavior when SalkindSchedule == 0x02.
Run primary U8P_SALKIND::schedule behavior when SalkindSchedule == 0x01.
Trigger follow-up area/U8P_SALKIND::schedule behavior after barking (call named as func021B which matches U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea process_type 0x021B; mapping inferred).
Invoke short targeted monologue when a specific candidate index is found and pathfind succeeds.
Invoke general idle monologue when pathfind succeeds and somebodyTalking is false.
Not typically invoked directly by U8P_SALKIND::use in this excerpt, but present as an idle helper called elsewhere; included for completeness.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether to U8P_SALKIND::use dead-phrasing for the description.
Clear internal script references (0x0205,0x0204,0x00F0,0x0200) on fast-area entry.
Clear internal script references on fast-area exit.
Prepare/orient NPC before speaking in the theft reaction.
Notify/trigger guard response as part of theft/aggro flow (indirectly via called helpers).
Spawn a short timer/helper used to wait/retry while no target is present.
Determine if the NPC currently has a target to drive reaction flow.
Shared reaction/bookkeeping call before guard notification when NPC has a valid target.
Alert guards when NPC has been targeted/attacked.
Test reachability to candidate target/item before proceeding with action or monologue.
Check the result of the suspended pathfind call to choose success/failure branches.
Schedule delayed retries or post-success follow-up timers.
Prepare/orient NPC before speaking in randomized alternate U8P_SALKIND::schedule branches.
Invoke reaction bookkeeping in certain randomized branches.
Spawn timers to pace the randomized sequence.
Measure distance to choose which set of barks/behavior to run.
Check item state (bitmask) to gate certain close-range dialogs.
Select among multiple idle barks randomly.
Spawn an action to have the NPC U8P_SALKIND::use/open a door referenced by a local handle after barking.
Reset conversation/reference state before building the menu.
Choose among alternate greeting lines when salkindPissed is set.
Randomly choose greeting variants and menu-related lines.
Check current map to tailor greeting/flow for map-specific lines.

reads.name: SalkindSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Selects which U8P_SALKIND::schedule helper to run on area entry (0x01 -> U8P_SALKIND::func04B6, 0x02 -> U8P_SALKIND::func05F7).

reads.name: toranDead
reads.likely_effect: Gates area-entry behavior and conversation branches; when set it prevents certain U8P_SALKIND::schedule/entry actions.

reads.name: secondexecution
reads.likely_effect: Prevents repeated execution of U8P_SALKIND::enterFastArea U8P_SALKIND::schedule behavior (execution guard).

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: If true, prevents utilities (U8P_SALKIND::func04B6) from interrupting with idle dialog (used to avoid overlapping conversations).

reads.name: salkindPissed
reads.likely_effect: Modifies greetings and conversation menu options when NPC is angry (read to pick angry greetings and menu entries).

reads.name: salkindMet
reads.likely_effect: Determines whether introductory 'name' flow is offered; read to decide which menu items/greetings to show.

reads.name: devonJailed
reads.likely_effect: Used to gate location-specific lines and whether Devon/Bentic topics are added to conversation.

reads.name: metDevonInJail
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether additional Devon/Bentic dialog options are offered.

reads.name: torwinMet
reads.likely_effect: Used to select alternate lines in idle monologue branches.

writes.name: salkindPissed
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the avatar steals from Salkind to mark an angry/alert state and change later dialog/behavior.

writes.name: salkindMet
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player asks Salkind's name; used to alter future conversation options and lines.

writes.name: salkindName
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player learns Salkind's name (tracks name revelation for U8P_SALKIND::look/menus); format/type inferred (likely boolean/presence).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements Salkind, an NPC with: a 'U8P_SALKIND::look' short-description, area-triggered idle behavior/schedules, reactions to theft and being targeted, and an interactive conversation menu.
